title: Cirrus: Add netavark/aardvark system test task

comments.body: I think you have to remove the `99-do-not-use-google-subnets.conflist` file, otherwise the network backend detection logic thinks that you want to use CNI because you already have extra CNI networks.

> already have extra CNI networks.

Oh gosh @Luap99 thank you so much.  I've been struggling to figure this out.  Oddly enough, when I go in and run the test manually, it works (after running the exact same `setup_environment.sh`).  I'll try what you suggest though, it's a good idea...

Still all-red CI, so I'm not likely to be able to review this before the weekend. But should you be interested, here's the new task map:
![cirrus-map-pr13024](https://user-images.githubusercontent.com/265639/151441357-1a47dfca-9f01-43a1-ba39-5cb34af795ec.png)
The new task is the pukey-lime one near six o'clock. I'm curious about its location: shouldn't it come after integration tests?

>  I'm not likely to be able to review

I think that's okay, I'm questioning if I can even get it working.  It's working fine under `hack/get_ci_vm.sh` but not normally...very strange since the environments are 99.999% the same (by design).

> shouldn't it come after integration tests?

The task map is going to be all f'd up while me `DO NOT MERGE` commit is in place.  It's messing with things to get the new task running as soon/quickly as possible.

@cevich please make sure there are issues filed for the problems, then:
```bash
    if running-under-netavark; then
      skip "FIXME: this test doesn't work under nevavark; #12345"
   fi
```
(or, if just skipping a portion of a test, include FIXME and #12345 on the same line in a comment. That makes it easy for my `find-obsolete-skips` script to alert me about these when #12345 gets fixed)

First test aardvark doesn't seem to work, we have to investigate why.
The second one is expected because why need to change the iptables command to flush netavark rules.

Great, thanks @Luap99 and @edsantiago .  Yep, I've already got an `is_netavark()` function ready.  I'll make sure to open issues on anything I mark `skip()` with it (seems like just two things - which is pretty good, all things considered).

[Clean run after skipping the two failing tests (I opened issues for them)](https://cirrus-ci.com/task/5179305075408896).  Rebased, squashed, and force-pushed w/o the `DO NOT MERGE` commit.  Fingers crossed :crossed_fingers:
Giving @Luap99 network-reload test fix suggestion a try.
[Example of netavark/aardvark build details.](https://cirrus-ci.com/task/5944241670062080?logs=package_versions#L16-L29)

Shouldn't the new task include `CI:BUILD`? Also, would it make sense to drag it up and run after `netavark_integration_test` instead of `local_integration_test`? Just would seem to look cleaner on the task map that way.
![cirrus-map-pr13024](https://user-images.githubusercontent.com/265639/152243446-0dabf98a-4b57-4507-b091-0747ba5b9e47.png)
Oh, good catches @edsantiago fixing that now...
It's really obvious in the diagram :-)
Done.  I also re-arranged the dependency list in `success` to match the task definition order.

That looks better, thanks. New map shows it in fuchsia near top right. 
![cirrus-map-pr13024](https://user-images.githubusercontent.com/265639/152244947-d2f318ec-3fe8-4a20-8e92-4ef0c7bb13bb.png)

Looks like I should've tested my suggestion, sorry:
```
# helpers.bash: line 347: test: =~: binary operator expected
```
Mass-re-run failed tasks: Something broke with quay.
I've been fighting what I think are CDN failures all day. Maybe they're related.

> CDN failures

IKR.  It's like the *ONE THING* we have CDNs for, and they're somehow not redundant enough (or some network broke)  :cry:
Just looked in Cirrus WebUI and things are progressing now (despite what the github UI says).
Yay :partying_face: quick merge it before the universe changes it's mind :smile:
/lgtm
/lgtm
/approve
